opp.name: NHS BSA
opp.next_step: Spoken with EY on this today we are going to spend time with them on the 22nd onsite Executive stakeholder alignment continuation. EY engagements early introductions and workshop participation View: Champion (Darren) established and tested. Requested internal info and intro to execs and project co-ordination. Ability to influence the deal and co-enable budget request re KPI and ROI/ business case jointly built. Aligned with Partner EY. This is well positioned as early positioning and assisting prospect to get budget and business case/ shaping RFP etc...
opp.ais_why:
No Avoma calls are associated with this opportunity, so conversation-level AI signal is unavailable.

Salesforce shows only two content-less tasks ('Onsite','Onsite Meeting') in 90 days and a Description centred on NHS payments and incumbent 'Arriba'—no AI, Merlin or agentic interest expressed.

Single stakeholder (Chief Digital, Data and Technology Officer) with one campaign touch; no demo or AI-capability exploration evidenced. Low confidence given absent call data.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (business case reference, no detail)
No Avoma calls
No Customer_Business_Problem__c or Value_to_Customer__c fields populated
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case is documented in Salesforce or call history. Next Step mentions 'KPI and ROI/business case jointly built' with the champion and the partner, but no specifics are captured (no cost savings target, cycle time improvement, error reduction, or headcount impact). The initiative appears to be pre-budget, with Darren Curry working to secure internal funding, but the business case content itself is absent. Without metrics, the deal lacks a defensible value anchor and cannot credibly progress to shortlist or vendor selection. The immediate move is to reconstruct the quantified case with Darren and document it.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (Darren named as champion, January context)
OpportunityContactRole (Darren, Influencer)
LastActivityDate 28 Jan 2026, no recent contact
champion.narrative: Darren Curry (Chief Digital, Data and Technology Officer) is identified as champion in Next Step, described as 'established and tested' with ability to influence budget, co-enable ROI case, and provide internal info and exec introductions. His engagement in January 2026 was active (requested intros, co-built business case). However, contact has been silent for 149 days (last activity 28 Jan 2026), and no recent follow-up is logged. Darren's current engagement status is unknown: he may still be working the internal budget process with EY, or the initiative may have stalled. His access to the Economic Buyer is implied but not demonstrated. The champion relationship is at risk due to the 5-month silence and must be reactivated immediately.

sources: SF Competitors__c: null
No competitive mentions in Next_Step__c, tasks, or fields
No Avoma calls
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. No competitor is named in Competitors__c, Next Step, tasks, or any Salesforce field. No Avoma calls exist to surface competitive shortlist. Next Step describes the deal as 'early positioning and assisting prospect to get budget and business case / shaping RFP', suggesting the buyer has not yet run a formal vendor evaluation or named alternatives. In a public-sector NHS procurement, competitors will emerge at RFP stage and likely include SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, or other G-Cloud-listed P2P platforms, plus incumbent systems. The partner EY may provide competitive intel, but none is recorded. Zycus is positioning early to shape requirements before competition formalizes, which is strategically sound, but the competitive landscape is a blind spot until the RFP process begins.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (business case work in progress, no pain detail)
No pain or compelling event fields populated
No Avoma calls
identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational or financial pain is not documented. Next Step references 'assisting prospect to get budget and business case', implying Darren Curry has identified internal inefficiency or spend leakage that justifies the investment, but the pain itself is not named. No Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, or Compelling_Event__c content exists. The 13-product scope suggests broad procurement and sourcing transformation (tail-spend, supplier risk, contract compliance, AI-driven analytics via Merlin), but without a named pain owner, quantified impact, or compelling event, the urgency is unclear. The champion is working to build the case, but the underlying pain narrative is missing. Reconstructing it with Darren is the immediate priority.

sources: No contracting or legal fields populated
No InfoSec or procurement documentation
Partner role (EY) unclear
paper_process.narrative: Paper process is unknown. No MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement channel mechanics are documented. As an NHS public-sector deal, contracting will likely require G-Cloud framework alignment, OJEU compliance if above threshold, and NHS Digital security assurance (DSPT, Cyber Essentials Plus), but none of this is mapped. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function. The partner EY may run procurement mechanics, but their exact role (implementation, advisory, resale) is unclear. Until the deal reaches formal evaluation or vendor selection, paper process is premature, but the gap will become critical at Shortlisted or later.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (requested exec intro, not completed)
OpportunityContactRole: zero EB/Exec Sponsor roles
No Who_will_approve_budget__c or X10a_Sponsor__c populated
economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is unidentified. Next Step references 'intro to execs' as a requested action from the champion Darren Curry, implying executive budget authority sits above Darren (Chief Digital, Data and Technology Officer), but no exec sponsor or CFO-level contact is named or logged. With a £500k deal requiring budget approval in a public-sector NHS organization, the EB is almost certainly at Director or C-suite level, likely requiring Treasury or Board sign-off. No Contact Role for Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor exists, and no commercial or pricing conversation is on record. The deal cannot progress to Shortlisted or beyond without mapping and engaging the EB.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (budget request and RFP shaping in progress)
No process or mandate fields populated
No Avoma calls to validate
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is inferred but not confirmed. Next Step indicates the deal is in early positioning mode, with the champion Darren Curry working to secure budget and shape the RFP before formal evaluation begins. The partner (EY) is positioned as co-enabler, suggesting a consulting-led or advisory engagement that may influence vendor selection. However, no explicit process map exists: no timeline for budget approval, RFP release, shortlist, or selection is documented. No What_is_the_decision_process__c or Mandate__c content. The typical NHS procurement cycle for a £500k+ technology buy involves internal business case, Treasury approval, OJEU-compliant tendering, and multi-stage evaluation, but none of this is confirmed. The deal is stuck pre-process, awaiting budget and formal kickoff.

sources: SF Products__c (13 modules listed, no use-case tie)
SF Next_Step__c (RFP shaping mentioned as future)
No Business_Requirements__c or Decision Criteria fields
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are unknown. No RFP, RFI, BRD, or structured evaluation framework is documented. Next Step mentions 'shaping RFP' as future work, indicating the buyer has not yet formalized requirements. No Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, or AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c content exists. The 13-product scope (iSource, ML iAnalyze, iManage, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, AppXtend, Agentic AI, ANA) is listed but not tied to specific use cases, fit criteria, or weighted selection factors. Without criteria, Zycus cannot position differentiation or validate shoe-fit. The next step is to co-develop the evaluation framework with Darren and the partner before the RFP is released.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Developing champion (Darren) co-built a business case, and EY is positioning early to shape the requirements
Pre-RFP with no EB, no pain confirmed, and a large ($500K) 13-product scope still to be defined

No direct meetings on record and the last activity was back in January; pre-RFP

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. No AIS Score or AIS Status in Salesforce. The product scope includes Merlin, Agentic AI, and ANA, suggesting Zycus proposed AI-driven analytics and automation, but no buyer feedback, appetite, or prioritization is documented. NHS organizations vary widely in digital maturity; some are AI-forward, others risk-averse. Without call content or a documented conversation, AI fit is a blind spot.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 23 Jan 2026, qualified immediately, then went silent after 28 Jan 2026. Next Step describes partner EY engagement, on-site meeting 'on the 22nd', executive alignment, and co-building business case with champion Darren, but no recent activity logged in 149 days. Last modified 16 Jun 2026 (10 days ago) but change content unknown. No field history or task progression visible.

items.name: Darren Curry
items.risk: High risk: champion has gone dark for 5 months, engagement status unknown, may have deprioritized or stalled internally
items.title: Chief Digital, Data and Technology Officer
items.sentiment: Positive in January 2026 (co-built business case, requested exec intros), but current sentiment unknown after 149-day silence

items.detail: Deal has been dark for 149 days with no buyer engagement and 157 days to a November 2026 close. The close date is almost certainly a placeholder and indefensible. If the buyer has not yet secured budget or released the RFP, the realistic timeline is 12-18 months from budget approval, pushing close to mid-2027 or later.

Single-threaded on one champion (Darren Curry) who has gone silent. No relationship with the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, procurement lead, or any other stakeholder. If Darren has deprioritized, left, or lost internal support, the deal is lost.

Next Step indicates the deal is pre-budget, with Darren working to secure internal funding and build the business case. If the budget request is rejected or deprioritized by NHS BSA Treasury or Board, the deal dies. No visibility into budget approval status or timeline.

Partner EY is referenced as co-enabler and joint workshop participant in Next Step, but no documented EY activity exists in Salesforce (no joint tasks, no calls, no email trail). If EY has disengaged or the advisory engagement ended, Zycus loses its inside track and early-positioning advantage.

Darren requested 'intro to execs' in January to map the Economic Buyer and secure executive sponsorship, but the intro was never completed (no EB Contact Role added, no exec engagement logged). Without executive alignment, a £500k deal in a public-sector organization cannot progress.

champion_strength.summary: Darren Curry was a developing champion in January 2026, actively co-building the business case with Zycus and EY, requesting internal info and exec introductions, and demonstrating influence over the budget process. However, the relationship has gone silent for 149 days with no logged contact, and his current engagement is unknown. The champion is at high risk of cooling or being lost entirely.

items.action: Re-engage Darren Curry directly to confirm budget status and RFP timeline.
items.trigger: Darren went silent after 28 January 2026, 149 days ago, following active co-building of business case and request for exec introductions.
items.expected_effect: Reactivates the champion relationship, clarifies whether the budget request is live or stalled, and re-establishes visibility into the deal timeline. If Darren replies, the deal can resume; if no reply in 10 business days, escalate through EY or alternate channel.

items.action: Secure introduction to the Economic Buyer and add to opportunity contacts.
items.trigger: Darren requested 'intro to execs' in January to map budget authority, but the introduction was never completed and no EB Contact Role exists.
items.expected_effect: Maps the exec sponsor or CFO-level budget authority required for a £500k NHS procurement, de-risks the single-threaded champion dependency, and enables direct commercial conversation with the decision-maker.

items.action: Reconstruct the quantified business case with Darren and document savings target.
items.trigger: Next Step describes 'KPI and ROI/business case jointly built' in January, but no metrics, cost savings, or value anchor is documented in Salesforce.
items.expected_effect: Provides the defensible ROI case required for NHS Treasury or Board budget approval, strengthens Darren's internal advocacy, and creates a tangible artifact to re-engage the EB and procurement stakeholders.

items.action: Confirm partner EY engagement status and next joint activity.
items.trigger: Next Step references EY as co-enabler and on-site meeting 'on the 22nd' in January, but no joint tasks, calls, or documented EY activity exists since then.
items.expected_effect: Validates whether EY is still actively engaged and can provide competitive intel, exec access, and procurement channel support. If EY has disengaged, Zycus loses its inside positioning and must rebuild buyer relationships directly.

items.action: Multi-thread to procurement and sourcing leads and add to stakeholder map.
items.trigger: Opportunity is single-threaded on Darren Curry (Influencer). Account-level contacts include Agbomeire Ikhaobomeh (Procurement and Contract Manager) and Angela Nixon-Moore (Strategic Sourcing Manager), but neither is connected to this opp.
items.expected_effect: De-risks the single-threaded champion dependency, builds relationships with the procurement and sourcing owners who will run the RFP process, and creates multiple pathways to re-engage if Darren remains unresponsive.

confidence_signals.summary: No confidence signals exist. The deal is dark, single-threaded, and has no MEDDPICC foundation, no buyer calls, and no recent activity. Every indicator points to high risk of loss or indefinite stall.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 23 Jan 2026, last buyer touch 28 Jan 2026 (149 days ago), 157 days to close 30 Nov 2026. Time-in-Qualified: ~5 months with no progression. No submission-date milestones recorded. Close date remains unchanged since creation, suggesting placeholder. At current pace (no movement in 149 days), the deal cannot credibly close by November.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains dark for 149 days since January 28 with zero buyer contact, no champion engagement, and no stage progression from Qualified in five months. No credible path to November close without immediate re-engagement.

best_practice_check.summary: Partner-led deal requires immediate re-engagement to prevent loss. Single-threaded on champion who has gone dark. No MEDDPICC foundation (EB, metrics, criteria) for a £500k, 13-module transformation.

competitive_position.summary: No competitors identified. The deal is pre-RFP, with Zycus and partner EY positioning early to shape requirements and secure budget before formal procurement begins. Once the NHS releases the RFP, competitors will likely include SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, and incumbent systems on the G-Cloud framework. Current threat level is unknown due to lack of recent intel.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is implied by the product scope (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA modules included) but not validated with the buyer. No AIS Score, no call content discussing AI appetite, and no documented use case for Merlin intake automation or agentic negotiation. Next Step describes early positioning and RFP shaping, which is the right moment to embed AI/Merlin differentiation, but whether Darren or NHS BSA prioritize AI is unknown. Under-positioned until the AI narrative is explicitly sold and tied to a quantified ROI.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations are unclear. The 13-product scope (full Source-to-Pay suite plus Merlin AI and Agentic AI) suggests comprehensive procurement transformation ambition, but no use cases, pain points, or fit criteria are documented. Next Step indicates Darren and EY are shaping the RFP and business case, implying Zycus is positioned to influence requirements, but the buyer's actual evaluation priorities are unknown.
